subject: Physics
text: A particle moves along a circular path of radius R from point A to point B, covering a semicircular arc. What are the path length and the magnitude of displacement, respectively?
explanation: Path length is the actual distance travelled along the semicircle, which is πR. Displacement is the shortest straight-line distance between the initial and final positions across the diameter, which is 2R.

subject: Physics
text: Which of the following statements regarding path length and displacement is NOT correct?

options.text: Path length is a scalar quantity, whereas displacement is a vector quantity.
The magnitude of displacement can be greater than the path length for a moving object.
Displacement can be positive, negative, or zero.
Path length is always positive or zero for a moving body.

explanation: Path length is always greater than or equal to the magnitude of displacement. The magnitude of displacement can never exceed the path length.

subject: Physics
text: A car travels the first half of a total distance d at a constant speed v₁ and the second half at a constant speed v₂. What is the average speed of the car for the entire journey?
explanation: Average speed = Total distance / Total time = d / (t₁ + t₂). Since t₁ = (d/2)/v₁ and t₂ = (d/2)/v₂, average speed = d / [(d/2v₁) + (d/2v₂)] = 2v₁v₂ / (v₁ + v₂).

subject: Physics
text: Under what condition is the magnitude of average velocity equal to the average speed of an object?

options.text: When the object moves along a curved path without changing direction.
When the object moves along a straight line in a fixed direction.
When the object moves in a circle with constant speed.
When the object reverses its direction of motion along a straight line.

explanation: When an object moves along a straight line in a single direction without turning back, the path length equals the magnitude of displacement, making average speed equal to average velocity magnitude.

subject: Physics
text: The position x of a particle moving along the x-axis varies with time t as x(t) = 3t² - 5t + 2, where x is in meters and t is in seconds. The instantaneous velocity of the particle at t = 2 s is:
explanation: Velocity v = dx/dt = d(3t² - 5t + 2)/dt = 6t - 5. Substituting t = 2 s gives v = 6(2) - 5 = 7 m/s.

subject: Physics
text: On a position-time (x-t) graph of a particle, what does the slope of the tangent at any given instant represent?
explanation: The slope of the tangent to the x-t graph at any instant gives the rate of change of position with respect to time, which is the instantaneous velocity v = dx/dt.

subject: Physics
text: A particle moves in a straight line with a constant acceleration of 2 m/s². If its initial velocity is 4 m/s, what is its velocity at t = 3 s?
explanation: Using the kinematic equation v = u + at: v = 4 + 2(3) = 10 m/s.

subject: Physics
topic: Kinematic Equations for Uniformly Accelerated Motion
text: A vehicle starting from rest moves with a uniform acceleration of 3 m/s² along a straight track. The distance covered by it in the first 4 seconds is:

subject: Physics
topic: Kinematic Equations for Uniformly Accelerated Motion
text: A bullet enters a wooden block with a speed of 20 m/s and comes to rest after penetrating a distance of 0.1 m. Assuming uniform retardation, the magnitude of its acceleration is:
explanation: Using v² = u² + 2ax with v = 0, u = 20 m/s, and x = 0.1 m: 0 = (20)² + 2a(0.1) ⇒ 0.2a = -400 ⇒ a = -2000 m/s². The magnitude of retardation is 2000 m/s².

subject: Physics
text: Two trains A and B of length 100 m each are moving on parallel tracks in the same direction with uniform speeds of 20 m/s and 15 m/s, respectively. The relative velocity of train A with respect to train B is:
explanation: Relative velocity v_AB = v_A - v_B = 20 m/s - 15 m/s = 5 m/s in the direction of motion.

subject: Physics
text: A runner completes one full round of a square track of side 50 m in 40 seconds. What will be the magnitude of displacement of the runner at the end of 2 minutes 20 seconds?
explanation: Total time = 2 min 20 s = 140 s. Number of rounds = 140 / 40 = 3.5 rounds. After 3.5 rounds, the runner is at the opposite corner of the square. Displacement = diagonal of square = √(50² + 50²) = 50√2 m.

subject: Physics
text:
An object moves along a straight line with velocity varying as follows:
• From 0–2 s, the velocity increases uniformly from 0 m/s to 10 m/s.
• From 2–4 s, the velocity decreases uniformly from 10 m/s to −10 m/s, crossing zero at t = 3 s.
• From 4–6 s, the velocity increases uniformly from −10 m/s to 0 m/s.
The total distance travelled by the object in 6 s is:
explanation:
The total distance equals the sum of the magnitudes of the areas under the velocity–time graph.
0–2 s: ½ × 2 × 10 = 10 m
2–4 s: Two equal triangles, each of area ½ × 1 × 10 = 5 m; total = 10 m
4–6 s: ½ × 2 × 10 = 10 m
Total distance = 10 + 10 + 10 = 30 m.

subject: Physics
text: A particle moves along a straight line such that its displacement x at time t is given by x² = t² + 1. The acceleration of the particle at any instant is proportional to:
explanation: Differentiating x² = t² + 1 gives 2x(dx/dt) = 2t ⇒ xv = t. Differentiating again: v + x(dv/dt) = 1 ⇒ x a = 1 - v². Substitute v = t/x: x a = 1 - (t²/x²) = (x² - t²)/x² = 1/x². Thus, acceleration a = 1/x³.

subject: Physics
topic: Kinematic Equations for Uniformly Accelerated Motion
text: A ball is dropped from a height H above the ground. It takes time T₁ to cover the first half of the distance and time T₂ to cover the second half. The ratio T₁/T₂ is:
explanation: For first half: H/2 = (1/2)g T₁² ⇒ T₁ = √(H/g). For total height: H = (1/2)g (T₁ + T₂)² ⇒ T₁ + T₂ = √(2H/g) = √2 T₁. Therefore, T₂ = (√2 - 1)T₁, which gives T₁/T₂ = 1 / (√2 - 1).

subject: Physics
topic: Kinematic Equations for Uniformly Accelerated Motion
text:
Assertion (A): For a body thrown vertically upwards, the acceleration at the highest point of its motion is zero.
Reason (R): At the highest point, the velocity of the body momentarily becomes zero.

options.text: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is the correct explanation of Assertion (A).
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true but Reason (R) is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion (A).
Assertion (A) is true but Reason (R) is false.
Assertion (A) is false but Reason (R) is true.

explanation: The acceleration throughout free fall is constant and equal to g (directed downwards), even at the highest point where velocity is momentarily zero. Thus, Assertion is false and Reason is true.

subject: Physics
topic: Kinematic Equations for Uniformly Accelerated Motion
text: Galileo’s law of odd numbers states that the distances traversed, during equal intervals of time, by a body falling freely from rest, stand to one another in the simple ratio of:
explanation: Galileo's law of odd numbers states that the distances covered by a body falling from rest in successive equal time intervals t are in the ratio of consecutive odd numbers, i.e., 1 : 3 : 5 : 7 : ...

subject: Physics
text: A stone is thrown vertically upward from the top of a tower with a speed of 10 m/s. It reaches the ground at the base of the tower in 4 seconds. The height of the tower is (take g = 10 m/s²):
explanation: Taking upward direction as positive: u = +10 m/s, a = -10 m/s², t = 4 s. Displacement y = ut + (1/2)at² = 10(4) + (1/2)(-10)(4)² = 40 - 80 = -40 m. The negative sign indicates displacement is downwards, so height of tower = 40 m.

subject: Physics
text: Two parallel rail tracks run north-south. Train A moves north with a speed of 54 km/h, and train B moves south with a speed of 90 km/h. What is the velocity of train B with respect to train A?
explanation: v_A = +54 km/h = +15 m/s (taking North as positive). v_B = -90 km/h = -25 m/s. Velocity of B with respect to A = v_B - v_A = -25 - 15 = -40 m/s, which means 40 m/s towards South.

subject: Physics
text: Which of the following position-time (x-t) graphs correctly represents a particle moving along a straight line with a constant negative acceleration?

options.text: A straight line sloping upwards with a positive gradient.
A horizontal straight line parallel to the time axis.

explanation: Since x = ut + (1/2)at², a constant negative acceleration (a < 0) gives a quadratic relationship where the curvature of the x-t graph is concave downwards (parabola opening downwards).

subject: Physics
topic: Kinematic Equations for Uniformly Accelerated Motion
text: A body starting from rest covers a distance S₁ in the first 2 seconds and a distance S₂ in the next 2 seconds under uniform acceleration. The relation between S₁ and S₂ is:
explanation: Distance in first 2 s: S₁ = (1/2)a(2)² = 2a. Distance in first 4 s: S_total = (1/2)a(4)² = 8a. Distance in next 2 s: S₂ = S_total - S₁ = 8a - 2a = 6a. Therefore, S₂ = 3S₁.

subject: Physics
text: The position x of a particle varies with time t according to the equation x = a t - b t³, where a and b are positive constants. The velocity of the particle when its acceleration becomes zero is:
explanation: Velocity v = dx/dt = a - 3bt². Acceleration accel = dv/dt = -6bt. Setting accel = 0 gives t = 0. Substituting t = 0 into velocity equation gives v = a - 3b(0)² = a.

subject: Physics
text: A body is thrown vertically upwards with velocity u. It passes a point P at height h above the ground at times t₁ and t₂ during its journey. Which of the following relationships is correct?
explanation: Using h = ut - (1/2)gt², we get quadratic equation gt² - 2ut + 2h = 0. The roots t₁ and t₂ represent times to reach height h. Product of roots t₁t₂ = 2h/g (and sum of roots t₁ + t₂ = 2u/g).

subject: Physics
topic: Kinematic Equations for Uniformly Accelerated Motion
text:
Assertion (A): The area under the acceleration-time (a-t) graph for a given time interval represents the change in velocity of the object.
Reason (R): Acceleration is defined as the rate of change of velocity with respect to time.

options.text: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is the correct explanation of Assertion (A).
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true but Reason (R) is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion (A).
Assertion (A) is true but Reason (R) is false.
Assertion (A) is false but Reason (R) is true.

explanation: Since a = dv/dt, integrating gives ∫ a dt = ∫ dv = Δv (change in velocity). The integral ∫ a dt is the area under the a-t graph, making Reason the correct explanation of Assertion.

subject: Physics
text: A police van moving on a highway with a speed of 36 km/h fires a bullet at a thief’s car speeding away in the same direction with a speed of 108 km/h. If the muzzle speed of the bullet is 140 m/s, with what speed does the bullet hit the thief’s car?
explanation: Speed of police van v_p = 36 km/h = 10 m/s. Muzzle speed of bullet = 140 m/s. Speed of bullet w.r.t ground v_b = 140 + 10 = 150 m/s. Speed of thief car v_t = 108 km/h = 30 m/s. Speed of bullet w.r.t thief car = v_b - v_t = 150 - 30 = 120 m/s.

subject: Physics
text: Which of the following speed-time (v-t) or position-time (x-t) graphs is physically IMPOSSIBLE for a real object moving in a straight line?

options.text: A position-time graph that shows two different positions for an object at the same instant of time.
A speed-time graph with a non-zero slope.
A position-time graph showing negative displacement values.
A velocity-time graph showing a sudden change in sign of velocity upon bouncing.

explanation: An object cannot occupy two different positions at the exact same instant of time, as that would imply infinite speed or teleportation, which is physically impossible.
